---
title: North Atlantic and Pacific Quasi‐Stationary Parts of Atmospheric Rivers and
  Their Implications for East Asian Monsoon Onset
pagetitle: 2019-11-1-Lee2019 - Gang Chen
body-classes: publication-page
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
year: 2019
author: Hung‐I Lee, Jonathan L Mitchell, Aradhna Tripati, Juan M Lora, Gang Chen and
  Qinghua Ding
venue: Geophysical Research Letters
venue_cit: <i>Geophysical Research Letters</i>, doi:10.1029/2019GL084272.
recommended_citation: 'Hung‐I Lee, Jonathan L Mitchell, Aradhna Tripati, Juan M Lora,
  Gang Chen and Qinghua Ding, 2019: North Atlantic and Pacific Quasi‐Stationary Parts
  of Atmospheric Rivers and Their Implications for East Asian Monsoon Onset, <i>Geophysical
  Research Letters</i>, doi:10.1029/2019GL084272.'
paper_url: https://doi.org/10.1029/2019GL084272
---

::: {.page-with-sidebar}

::: {.sidebar-col}
{{< include /_author-profile.qmd >}}
:::

::: {.content-col}

# North Atlantic and Pacific Quasi‐Stationary Parts of Atmospheric Rivers and Their Implications for East Asian Monsoon Onset

*Published in Geophysical Research Letters, 2019*

**Recommended citation:** Hung‐I Lee, Jonathan L Mitchell, Aradhna Tripati, Juan M Lora, Gang Chen and Qinghua Ding, 2019: North Atlantic and Pacific Quasi‐Stationary Parts of Atmospheric Rivers and Their Implications for East Asian Monsoon Onset, <i>Geophysical Research Letters</i>, doi:10.1029/2019GL084272.

[Download paper](https://doi.org/10.1029/2019GL084272){target="_blank"}

:::

:::
